Section Title: DSP MF Temporarily Restricts Subscription to Global Schemes: Here’s Why
Content:
These schemes, in addition to others, have been popular among Indian investors seeking exposure to international markets and reducing the overall risk in their portfolios through geographic diversification.
**[Read:** [Indian Mutual Funds May Soon Be Permitted to Invest in Overseas Funds](https://www.personalfn.com/mf-news/Indian-Mutual-Funds-May-Soon-Be-Permitted-to-Invest-in-Overseas-Funds) **]**
**Reason Behind the Temporary Restriction**
On a broader level, mutual fund houses can impose such restrictions for several reasons, ranging from regulatory changes to market conditions. DSP Mutual Fund’s decision to pause new subscriptions to its global schemes stems from the following factors:
**1. Regulatory Limits on Foreign Investments by Mutual Funds**
Section Title: DSP MF Temporarily Restricts Subscription to Global Schemes: Here’s Why
Content:
Indian mutual funds, including DSP Mutual Fund, operate under the regulations set forth by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) and the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). These regulatory bodies [cap the total amount Indian mutual funds can invest in overseas markets](https://www.personalfn.com/dwl/mutual-funds/sebi-raises-foreign-investment-limit-for-mutual-funds-what-should-your-international-fund-investment-strategy-be) . At present, this cap stands at USD 7 billion for mutual funds, with an additional USD 1 billion limit for investing in exchange-traded funds (ETFs) this rules came into effect from February 02, 2022.
Section Title: DSP MF Temporarily Restricts Subscription to Global Schemes: Here’s Why
Content:
The recent surge in demand for global investment products has led many mutual fund houses to hit this cap, or come close to doing so. DSP Mutual Fund is among those affected by this limitation, and the firm has no choice but to restrict new investments until more leeway is granted by regulators or market conditions change.
**2. Concerns Over Market Volatility**

Details here https://www.businesstoday.in/personal-finance/news/story/dsp-mutual-fund-temporarily-halts-new-investments-in-international-funds-details-here-448719-2024-10-04?utm_source=btweb_story_share "Whatsapp share")
The Budget 2024 has brought about changes to the tax implications and holding periods for Short Term Capital Gains (STCG) and Long Term Capital Gains (LTCG) in the context of international fund of funds.
DSP Mutual Fund has implemented a temporary suspension on specific international schemes to prevent exceeding regulatory limits on overseas investments. To adhere to these regulations, the fund house is currently not accepting lumpsum investments, new registrations for Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs), and Systematic Transfer Plans (STPs) in certain funds.
Section Title: ... > To adhere to these regulations, the fund house is currently not accepting lumpsum investmen...
Content:
Advertisement
These funds are:
DSP Global Innovation Fund of Fund
DSP Global Allocation Fund of Fund
DSP Global Clean Energy Fund of Fund
DSP World Agriculture Fund
DSP US Flexible Equity Fund of Fund
DSP World Gold Fund of Fund
DSP World Mining Fund
Existing SIPs and STPs in these funds will continue as usual.
As a preventive step, this action was taken to prevent exceeding the DSP Mutual Fund level limit as of February 01, 2022. Designated employees must follow clause 6.10 of the SEBI Master Circular when making additional investments, opting for schemes with risk values equal to or greater than the current schemes as per the risk-o-meter.
Section Title: ... > To adhere to these regulations, the fund house is currently not accepting lumpsum investmen...
Content:
According to SEBI, the total industry limit for overseas investment is set at US $7 billion and the limit for overseas Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) is set at US $1 billion. SEBI, in an email dated January 28, 2022, and AMFI, in an email dated January 30, 2022, have instructed Asset Management Companies (AMCs) to temporarily limit subscriptions in schemes that plan to invest in overseas securities starting from February 2, 2022. This measure is to ensure compliance with the industry-wide overseas investment limits allowed by the RBI.
Advertisement
In compliance with directives from SEBI and AMFI via emails dated March 19, 2024 and March 20, 2024 respectively, AMCs have been instructed to temporarily limit subscriptions in schemes investing in overseas Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) starting April 01, 2024 to prevent exceeding industry-wide investment limits in overseas ETFs.
Section Title: ... > To adhere to these regulations, the fund house is currently not accepting lumpsum investmen...
Content:
As a precautionary measure to prevent breaching the DSP MF level limit as of February 01, 2022, temporary restrictions have been implemented by DSP Mutual Fund. Any requests for lump sum subscriptions, switch-ins, new SIP/STP/IDCW Transfer Plan registrations in the DSP Mutual Fund's international schemes after the cut-off time on October 1, 2024, will not be accepted.
Nevertheless, SIP/STP installments for existing SIP/STP registrations in designated schemes as of October 1, 2024, will continue until further notice.

Section Title: ... > International mutual funds investing in foreign assets must adhere to the overseas investme...
Content:
[Follow Us](https://news.google.com/publications/CAAqBwgKMOeb3gow873VAQ?hl=en-IN&gl=IN&ceid=IN%3Aen)
SEBI had specified an overall industry limits for overseas investment and overseas Exchange Traded Fund. (Reuters)
DSP [Mutual Fund](https://www.financialexpress.com/market/mutual-funds/) has temporarily restricted subscriptions to units of seven schemes that hold global assets in their portfolio. The schemes that are impacted include DSP Global Innovation Fund of Fund, DSP Global Allocation Fund of Fund, DSP Global Clean Energy Fund of Fund, DSP World Agriculture Fund, DSP US Flexible [Equity Fund](https://www.financialexpress.com/market/mutual-funds/equity-funds/) of Fund, DSP World Gold Fund of Fund and DSP World Mining Fund
Section Title: ... > International mutual funds investing in foreign assets must adhere to the overseas investme...
Content:
International mutual funds that invest in foreign assets such as equity shares, bonds, or gold on behalf of Indian investors have to adhere to overseas investment limits as set by the market regulator, [SEBI](https://financialexpress.com/about/sebi/) .
SEBI had specified an overall industry limit for overseas investment as US $7 billion and overseas Exchange Traded Fund (ETF(s) limit as US $1 billion. These rules came into force on February 2, 2022, to avoid breach of industry-wide overseas investment limits as permitted by [RBI](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/rbi/) .
Further, in June 2022, AMFI advised MF houses that Mutual Fund schemes may resume subscriptions and make investments in overseas funds/securities upto the headroom available without breaching the overseas investment limits as of end of February 01, 2022 at Mutual Fund level.
Section Title: ... > International mutual funds investing in foreign assets must adhere to the overseas investme...
Content:
Later, SEBI vide email dated March 19, 2024 and AMFI vide email dated March 20, 2024, directed AMCs to temporarily restrict the subscription in the schemes that intend to invest in overseas Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) w.e.f. April 01, 2024 to avoid breach of industry-wide limits for investment in overseas ETFs.
Now, as a precautionary measure, to avoid the breach of DSP MF level limit as on February 01, 2022, the temporary restrictions are in place by the DSP Mutual Fund.
Any lump sum subscription, switch-in, new SIP/STP/ IDCW Transfer Plan registration requests received in the DSP Mutual Fund’s international schemes post the cut-off timing on October 1, 2024, are not being accepted.
However, SIP/STP installments for existing SIP/STP registration in Designated Schemes as on October 1, 2024, will continue till further notice.
